hypothesis: Deploying SAM-EM in a closed-loop LPTEM workflow that reduces electron dose rate in real time whenever the model detects signatures of beam-induced perturbation (e.g., abrupt decreases in MSD or onset of sub-diffusive motion) will reduce the frequency of beam-induced nanoparticle aggregation events by at least 30% compared to identical open-loop experiments with constant dose under the same imaging conditions (35 e−/Å2·s, 80 fps) and liquid thicknesses of 50–150 nm.
